title: Finite Cyclic Geometry Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite cyclic-geometry diagonal-intersection, angle-dot, and Ptolemy QF_LRA/Farkas regression seeds
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact cyclic-quadrilateral replay computes the square's diagonal intersection x-coordinate as 0 while one malformed row claims x-coordinate 1/2, computes the opposite-angle dot product at B as 0 while another row claims 1, and computes the Ptolemy right-hand side for a 4-by-3 rectangle as 25 while another row claims 24.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional cyclic-geometry rows only when they add distinct nontrivial circle-line correspondence, higher-degree polynomial-geometry, or theorem-reconstruction pressure; keep general cyclic-quadrilateral theorems in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: Encode fixed rational cyclic-geometry rows as deterministic exact-coordinate replay obligations.
Replay point-on-circle, diagonal-intersection, diagonal-perpendicularity, opposite-angle, and rational Ptolemy witnesses over exact rationals.

Route rejected finite diagonal-intersection, opposite-angle dot-product, and Ptolemy claims through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact coordinate, dot product, or product-sum value.
Keep general cyclic-quadrilateral theorems, angle-chasing, and circle-line correspondence marked Lean-horizon until proof artifacts exist.
